hooks.prompt: Review the tRPC code change for issues. Check for: 1) Procedure without input validation schema (accepts any input — always define z.object() input schema with strict validation on every procedure), 2) Missing error handling or using generic throw instead of TRPCError (unhelpful error messages — throw new TRPCError({ code: 'NOT_FOUND', message: '...' }) with appropriate code), 3) Query procedure that mutates data (semantic violation — queries should be GET-equivalent and idempotent; use mutation for state changes), 4) Missing middleware for auth checks on protected routes (unprotected endpoints — create protectedProcedure with auth middleware that verifies session/token), 5) Router with all procedures in a single file (unmaintainable — split into domain routers and merge with mergeRouters or router nesting), 6) Missing optimistic updates on mutations in React Query integration (UI feels slow — use utils.model.setData for instant feedback, revert on error), 7) Subscription without cleanup or error reconnection logic (memory leaks, dropped connections — implement onComplete cleanup and automatic reconnection with backoff), 8) Output schema not defined (response shape not validated — add .output(z.object({...})) for runtime type safety on responses), 9) Context creation with expensive operations (runs on every request — move DB connections and heavy initialization outside createContext, use lazy evaluation), 10) Missing batching configuration or batching disabled for operations that should batch (multiple round trips — enable httpBatchLink and configure maxURLLength for query batching). Return {"ok": true} if no issues, or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issue found"} if problems detected.
